title: Rulewright rule document
description: A Rulewright rule document: either a single rule or a rule set. Logic lives in 'condition' and 'actions'; the 'layout' key is presentation metadata for canvas tools and is ignored entirely by the engine.

decisionTable.description: A tabular authoring form that expands into ordinary rules — one rule per row. Each input column contributes a condition (a null cell is a wildcard); a row with all-wildcard inputs is a catch-all. Each output column contributes an action.

id.description: Base for generated row rule ids ('<id>-<rowIndex>'); defaults to 'row'.

name.description: Optional display name for the expanded rule set.

hitPolicy.description: 'collect' (default): every matching row applies its actions in row order. 'first': only the first matching row (in row order) applies.

inputs.description: Input columns, left to right; each maps a 'when' cell to a condition.

outputs.description: Output columns, left to right; each maps a 'then' cell to an action.

field.description: Dotted fact path this column compares against.

operator.description: Comparison applied to non-wildcard cells in this column.

type.description: How a non-null 'then' cell combines into the outputs.

when.description: One cell per input column. A null cell is a wildcard (no condition). For In/NotIn columns a cell is an array; otherwise a scalar.

then.description: One cell per output column. A null cell skips that output for this row; any other value is an expression.

name.description: Optional display name for the rule set.

rules.description: The rules in this set. Evaluation order is by descending 'priority', then document order.

id.description: Unique identifier for the rule within its set.

description.description: Human-readable summary of what the rule does.

priority.description: Higher-priority rules are evaluated first; ties keep document order.

enabled.description: Disabled rules are skipped entirely during evaluation.

actions.description: Actions applied when the condition evaluates to true.

layout.description: Presentation/layout metadata for visual rule builders (node positions, canvas ids). The engine never reads this key, and it is excluded from the rule content hash used for compilation caching.

operator.description: Logical combinator. AND/OR take one or more child conditions and short-circuit left to right; NOT takes exactly one child.

field.description: Dotted path resolved against the fact, e.g. 'Customer.Age'.

value.description: Comparison operand. Type depends on the operator: scalar for comparisons, string for string operators, array for In/NotIn, forbidden for IsNull/IsNotNull.

name.description: Registered custom function name; required when operator is 'custom'.

type.description: How the value combines into the result's outputs at 'target': 'setOutput' replaces, 'addToOutput' adds numerically (a running total across fired rules), 'appendToOutput' appends to a list (collected across fired rules). For the accumulators, a null value contributes nothing.

value.description: The value written to the output key: a constant scalar, or a value expression computed from the fact at evaluation time.

expression.description: An output value. Pure data — a closed operator vocabulary, never embedded code. A bare scalar is a constant; object nodes are { 'literal': <scalar> }, { 'field': '<dotted path>' }, or { 'op': '<operator>', 'operands': [ <expression>, ... ] }. Operand order is significant for subtract, divide, and modulo.

literal.description: An explicit constant, equivalent to writing the bare scalar.

field.description: Dotted path read from the fact, e.g. 'Order.Total'. A null anywhere along the path yields null.

op.description: Arithmetic (add/subtract/multiply/divide/modulo/negate), string concatenation (concat), or first-non-null selection (coalesce). Any null operand propagates to null (except coalesce); division or modulo by zero yields null.

operands.description: Operand expressions. negate takes exactly 1; subtract/divide/modulo take exactly 2; add/multiply/concat/coalesce take 2 or more.
